commit_message:
[Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend [Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend This PR adds partial support for Verilog FP units. I used the setup [here](https://github.com/Jiahui17/xls-float-ip/tree/main) to generate the FP units from XLS

I will open a separate issue extension for this.

fixes #238

examples.commit_message:
[Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend [Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend This PR adds partial support for Verilog FP units. I used the setup [here](https://github.com/Jiahui17/xls-float-ip/tree/main) to generate the FP units from XLS

I will open a separate issue extension for this.

fixes #238
examples.patch:
@@ -17,73 +17,18 @@ module addf #(
   output rhs_ready
 );
   
-  // Assert that DATA_TYPE is 32
-  //assert(DATA_TYPE == 32) else $error("addf currently only supports 32-bit floating point operands");
-  
-  wire join_valid, oehb_ready, buff_valid;
-  wire constant_zero = 1'b0;
-  wire open_value;
-
-  // intermediate input signals for IEEE-754 to Flopoco-simple-float conversion
-  wire [ DATA_TYPE + 1 :0] ip_lhs, ip_rhs;
-
-  // intermediate output signal for Flopoco-simple-float to IEEE-754 conversion
-  wire [ DATA_TYPE + 1 :0] ip_result;
-
-  // Instantiate the join node
-  join_type #(
-    .SIZE(2)
-  ) join_inputs (
-    .ins_valid  ({rhs_valid, lhs_valid}),
-    .outs_ready (oehb_ready             ),
-    .ins_ready  ({rhs_ready, lhs_ready}  ),
-    .outs_valid (join_valid             )
-  );
-
-  delay_buffer #(
-    .SIZE(8)
-  ) buff (
-    .clk(clk),
-    .rst(rst),
-    .valid_in(join_valid),
-    .ready_in(oehb_ready),
-    .valid_out(buff_valid)
-  );
-
-  oehb #(
-    .DATA_TYPE(1)
-  ) oehb_lhs (
+  __xls_float_ips__addf32_0_next ip (
     .clk(clk),
     .rst(rst),
-    .ins(constant_zero),
-    .ins_valid(buff_valid),
-    .ins_ready(oehb_ready),
-    .outs(open_value),
-    .outs_valid(result_valid),
-    .outs_ready(result_ready)
-  );
-
-  ieee2nfloat_lhs  InputIEEE_32bit (
-    .X(lhs),
-    .R(ip_lhs)
-  );
-
-  ieee2nfloat_rhs  InputIEEE_32bit (
-    .X(rhs),
-    .R(ip_rhs)
-  );
-
-  nfloat2ieee  OutputIEEE_32bit (
-    .X(ip_result),
-    .R(result)
-  );
-
-  ip  FloatingPointAdder (
-    .clk(clk),
-    .ce(oehb_ready),
-    .X(ip_lhs),
-    .Y(ip_rhs),
-    .R(ip_result)
+    .xls_float_ips__lhs(lhs),
+    .xls_float_ips__lhs_vld(lhs_valid),
+    .xls_float_ips__lhs_rdy(lhs_ready),
+    .xls_float_ips__rhs(rhs),
+    .xls_float_ips__rhs_vld(rhs_valid),
+    .xls_float_ips__rhs_rdy(rhs_ready),
+    .xls_float_ips__result(result),
+    .xls_float_ips__result_vld(result_valid),
+    .xls_float_ips__result_rdy(result_ready)
   );
 
-endmodule
\ No newline at end of file
+endmodule
examples.after_code:
`timescale 1ns/1ps
module addf #(
  parameter DATA_TYPE = 32
)(
  // inputs
  input  clk,
  input  rst,
  input  [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] lhs,
  input  lhs_valid,
  input  [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] rhs,
  input  rhs_valid,
  input  result_ready,
  // outputs
  output [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] result,
  output result_valid,
  output lhs_ready,
  output rhs_ready
);
  
  __xls_float_ips__addf32_0_next ip (
    .clk(clk),
    .rst(rst),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs(lhs),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs_vld(lhs_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs_rdy(lhs_ready),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs(rhs),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs_vld(rhs_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs_rdy(rhs_ready),
    .xls_float_ips__result(result),
    .xls_float_ips__result_vld(result_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__result_rdy(result_ready)
  );

endmodule

examples.commit_message:
[Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend [Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend This PR adds partial support for Verilog FP units. I used the setup [here](https://github.com/Jiahui17/xls-float-ip/tree/main) to generate the FP units from XLS

I will open a separate issue extension for this.

fixes #238
examples.after_code:
`timescale 1ns/1ps

module ENTITY_NAME #(
  parameter DATA_TYPE = 32
)(
  // inputs
  input  clk,
  input  rst,
  input  [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] lhs,
  input  lhs_valid,
  input  [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] rhs,
  input  rhs_valid,
  input  result_ready,
  // outputs
  output result,
  output result_valid,
  output lhs_ready,
  output rhs_ready
);

  __xls_float_ips__cmpf32_COMPARATOR_0_next ip (
    .clk(clk),
    .rst(rst),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs(lhs),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs_vld(lhs_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs_rdy(lhs_ready),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs(rhs),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs_vld(rhs_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs_rdy(rhs_ready),
    .xls_float_ips__result(result),
    .xls_float_ips__result_vld(result_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__result_rdy(result_ready)
  );

endmodule

examples.commit_message:
[Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend [Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend This PR adds partial support for Verilog FP units. I used the setup [here](https://github.com/Jiahui17/xls-float-ip/tree/main) to generate the FP units from XLS

I will open a separate issue extension for this.

fixes #238

@@ -17,74 +17,18 @@ module divf #(
   output rhs_ready
 );
 
-  //assert(DATA_TYPE == 32) else $error("divf currently only supports 32-bit floating point operands");
-
-  wire join_valid, oehb_ready, buff_valid;
-  wire constant_zero = 1'b0;
-  wire open_value;
-
-  // intermediate input signals for IEEE-754 to Flopoco-simple-float conversion
-  wire [ DATA_TYPE + 1 :0] ip_lhs, ip_rhs;
-
-  // intermediate output signal for Flopoco-simple-float to IEEE-754 conversion
-  wire [ DATA_TYPE + 1 :0] ip_result;
-
-  // Instantiate the join node
-  join_type #(
-    .SIZE(2)
-  ) join_inputs (
-    .ins_valid  ({rhs_valid, lhs_valid}),
-    .outs_ready (oehb_ready             ),
-    .ins_ready  ({rhs_ready, lhs_ready}  ),
-    .outs_valid (join_valid             )
-  );
-
-  delay_buffer #(
-    .SIZE(8)
-  ) buff (
+  __xls_float_ips__divf32_0_next ip (
     .clk(clk),
     .rst(rst),
-    .valid_in(join_valid),
-    .ready_in(oehb_ready),
-    .valid_out(buff_valid)
+    .xls_float_ips__lhs(lhs),
+    .xls_float_ips__lhs_vld(lhs_valid),
+    .xls_float_ips__lhs_rdy(lhs_ready),
+    .xls_float_ips__rhs(rhs),
+    .xls_float_ips__rhs_vld(rhs_valid),
+    .xls_float_ips__rhs_rdy(rhs_ready),
+    .xls_float_ips__result(result),
+    .xls_float_ips__result_vld(result_valid),
+    .xls_float_ips__result_rdy(result_ready)
   );
 
-  oehb #(
-    .DATA_TYPE(1)
-  ) oehb_lhs (
-    .clk(clk),
-    .rst(rst),
-    .ins(constant_zero),
-    .ins_valid(buff_valid),
-    .ins_ready(oehb_ready),
-    .outs(open_value),
-    .outs_valid(result_valid),
-    .outs_ready(result_ready)
-  );
-
-  ieee2nfloat_lhs  InputIEEE_32bit (
-    .X(lhs),
-    .R(ip_lhs)
-  );
-
-  ieee2nfloat_rhs  InputIEEE_32bit (
-    .X(rhs),
-    .R(ip_rhs)
-  );
-
-  nfloat2ieee  OutputIEEE_32bit (
-    .X(ip_result),
-    .R(result)
-  );
-
-  ip  FloatingPointDivider (
-    .clk(clk),
-    .ce(oehb_ready),
-    .X(ip_lhs),
-    .Y(ip_rhs),
-    .R(ip_result)
-  );
-
-
-
-endmodule
\ No newline at end of file
+endmodule

`timescale 1ns/1ps
module divf #(
  parameter DATA_TYPE = 32
)(
  // inputs
  input  clk,
  input  rst,
  input  [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] lhs,
  input  lhs_valid,
  input  [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] rhs,
  input  rhs_valid,
  input  result_ready,
  // outputs
  output [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] result,
  output result_valid,
  output lhs_ready,
  output rhs_ready
);

  __xls_float_ips__divf32_0_next ip (
    .clk(clk),
    .rst(rst),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs(lhs),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs_vld(lhs_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs_rdy(lhs_ready),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs(rhs),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs_vld(rhs_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs_rdy(rhs_ready),
    .xls_float_ips__result(result),
    .xls_float_ips__result_vld(result_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__result_rdy(result_ready)
  );

endmodule

examples.commit_message:
[Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend [Verilog] Partial Support for FP Unit in Verilog Backend This PR adds partial support for Verilog FP units. I used the setup [here](https://github.com/Jiahui17/xls-float-ip/tree/main) to generate the FP units from XLS

I will open a separate issue extension for this.

fixes #238
examples.patch:
@@ -17,77 +17,18 @@ module subf #(
   output rhs_ready
 );
 
-  //assert(DATA_TYPE == 32) else $fatal("subf currently only supports 32-bit operands");
-
-  wire join_valid, oehb_ready, buff_valid;
-  wire constant_zero = 1'b0;
-  wire open_value;
-
-  // subf is the same as addf, but we flip the sign bit of rhs
-  wire [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] rhs_neg;
-
-  // intermediate input signals for IEEE-754 to Flopoco-simple-float conversion
-  wire [ DATA_TYPE + 1 :0] ip_lhs, ip_rhs;
-
-  // intermediate output signal for Flopoco-simple-float to IEEE-754 conversion
-  wire [ DATA_TYPE + 1 :0] ip_result;
-
-  // Instantiate the join node
-  join_type #(
-    .SIZE(2)
-  ) join_inputs (
-    .ins_valid  ({rhs_valid, lhs_valid}),
-    .outs_ready (oehb_ready             ),
-    .ins_ready  ({rhs_ready, lhs_ready}  ),
-    .outs_valid (join_valid             )
-  );
-
-  delay_buffer #(
-    .SIZE(8)
-  ) buff (
-    .clk(clk),
-    .rst(rst),
-    .valid_in(join_valid),
-    .ready_in(oehb_ready),
-    .valid_out(buff_valid)
-  );
-
-  oehb #(
-    .DATA_TYPE(1)
-  ) oehb_lhs (
+  __xls_float_ips__subf32_0_next ip (
     .clk(clk),
     .rst(rst),
-    .ins(constant_zero),
-    .ins_valid(buff_valid),
-    .ins_ready(oehb_ready),
-    .outs(open_value),
-    .outs_valid(result_valid),
-    .outs_ready(result_ready)
-  );
-
-  ieee2nfloat_lhs  InputIEEE_32bit (
-    .X(lhs),
-    .R(ip_lhs)
-  );
-
-  assign rhs_neg = ~rhs + 1;
-
-  ieee2nfloat_rhs  InputIEEE_32bit (
-    .X(rhs_neg),
-    .R(ip_rhs)
-  );

examples.after_code:
`timescale 1ns/1ps
module subf #(
  parameter DATA_TYPE = 32
)(
  // inputs
  input  clk,
  input  rst,
  input  [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] lhs,
  input  lhs_valid,
  input  [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] rhs,
  input  rhs_valid,
  input  result_ready,
  // outputs
  output [DATA_TYPE - 1 : 0] result,
  output result_valid,
  output lhs_ready,
  output rhs_ready
);

  __xls_float_ips__subf32_0_next ip (
    .clk(clk),
    .rst(rst),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs(lhs),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs_vld(lhs_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__lhs_rdy(lhs_ready),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs(rhs),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs_vld(rhs_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__rhs_rdy(rhs_ready),
    .xls_float_ips__result(result),
    .xls_float_ips__result_vld(result_valid),
    .xls_float_ips__result_rdy(result_ready)
  );

endmodule
